educationalNotes.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
educationalNotes.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent layers.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions. Resizing inputs to 224x224 is required prior to forward propagation.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors in standard CNN dense layers.

calculationSteps.label: Kernel Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 32 filters of size 3x3 convolving over 3 RGB channels. Stride 2 halves resolution.

educationalNotes.summary: Standard early 3x3 convolution to downsample inputs.
educationalNotes.detailed: Computes 32 feature maps by sliding 3x3 filters with a stride of 2 across the raw RGB channels to capture early colors and edges.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Shrinks inputs early to decrease computational costs downstream.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Early layers extract basic visual primitives (edges, shapes).

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNotes.detailed: Calculates f(x) = min(max(0, x), 6). Capping the activation at 6 prevents values from expanding infinitely, which preserves precision during fixed-point quantization on edge devices.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Critical for low-power mobile CPU and DSP quantization compatibility.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: ReLU6 restricts activation range to support lightweight integer quantization.

calculationSteps.label: Depthwise weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Apply one 3x3 filter per channel independently (groups = channels), which separates spatial convolving from channel mixing.

educationalNotes.detailed: Applies a single 3x3 kernel per input channel. Standard convolutions mix spatial and channel details at the same time; depthwise convs only slide spatially. Saves over 90% parameter overhead compared to standard convs.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: The core mechanism behind MobileNet's fast runtime and low parameter size.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Depthwise convolutions perform spatial filtering without mixing channels.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNotes.summary: Applies ReLU6 activation to depthwise outputs.

calculationSteps.label: Projection weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Compresses channel information.

educationalNotes.detailed: Compresses 32 channels back to 16. Crucially, this layer does NOT apply a non-linear activation (it is linear). Non-linear activations on narrow channels throw away valuable visual information.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Preserves information capacity in low-dimensional spaces.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Linear bottlenecks prevent activation loss in compact channels.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 1x1 projection expanding channels.

educationalNotes.summary: 1x1 Expansion convolution projecting to higher dims.
educationalNotes.detailed: Projects input from 16 to a higher-dimensional space of 96 channels. Expands capacity so the subsequent depthwise conv can process complex filters.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: MobileNetV2 uses an 'Inverted Bottleneck' structure where filters are expanded first, then compressed.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Expansion makes learning high-dimensional manifolds easier.

educationalNotes.detailed: Adds the bottleneck block output to its input tensor. Enabled only when resolution and channels are identical.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Residual connections support deep information propagation.

calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

calculationSteps.label: Depthwise weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Apply one 3x3 filter per channel independently (groups = channels), which separates spatial convolving from channel mixing.

educationalNotes.detailed: Applies a single 3x3 kernel per input channel. Standard convolutions mix spatial and channel details at the same time; depthwise convs only slide spatially. Saves over 90% parameter overhead compared to standard convs.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: The core mechanism behind MobileNet's fast runtime and low parameter size.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Depthwise convolutions perform spatial filtering without mixing channels.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNotes.summary: Applies ReLU6 activation to depthwise outputs.

calculationSteps.label: Projection weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Compresses channel information.

educationalNotes.detailed: Compresses 576 channels back to 96. Crucially, this layer does NOT apply a non-linear activation (it is linear). Non-linear activations on narrow channels throw away valuable visual information.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Preserves information capacity in low-dimensional spaces.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Linear bottlenecks prevent activation loss in compact channels.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNotes.detailed: Adds the bottleneck block output to its input tensor. Enabled only when resolution and channels are identical.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Residual connections support deep information propagation.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 1x1 projection expanding channels.

educationalNotes.summary: 1x1 Expansion convolution projecting to higher dims.
educationalNotes.detailed: Projects input from 96 to a higher-dimensional space of 576 channels. Expands capacity so the subsequent depthwise conv can process complex filters.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: MobileNetV2 uses an 'Inverted Bottleneck' structure where filters are expanded first, then compressed.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Expansion makes learning high-dimensional manifolds easier.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Final 1x1 projection expanding descriptors.

educationalNotes.summary: 1x1 projection expanding visual features before pooling.
educationalNotes.detailed: Aggregates features to 1,280 channels before pooling to enrich the global visual classifier descriptor.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNotes.summary: Collapses 7x7 spatial layout into a global channel average vector.
educationalNotes.detailed: Averages pixel coordinates across each channel to form a final dense vector, reducing overfitting constraints.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: GAP drastically restricts parameter weights size.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Maps descriptors to classes.

calculationSteps.label: Biases
calculationSteps.explanation: Category offsets.

educationalNotes.summary: Dense predictions softmax layer resolving ImageNet classes.
